macro_context.headline: Trump rejects Iran counter-proposal, calls ceasefire 'on life support' — oil surges past $100, S&P futures slide ahead of Tuesday CPI.

sections.past: EUR/USD drifted lower to 1.1754 as the dollar firmed on safe-haven demand after Trump declared the Iran ceasefire 'on life support.'
sections.why: DXY reclaiming 98+ signals the market is repricing geopolitical risk premium back into the dollar — the ECB-hold vs. potential Fed-hawkishness gap is widening again.
sections.watch: Tuesday CPI at 15:30 TRT is the week's pivot — hot print above 3.5% YoY locks out June cuts and sends EUR toward 1.1700.

headlines.title: The Euro’s Next Move Will Make or Break FIDI’s 2026 Returns
Dollar Slides on Stock Strength and Weak Consumer Sentiment
Sterling Today: Pound jumps as dollar sells off on Iran-Hormuz deal hopes

sections.past: Cable sold off 60 pips to 1.3535 as sterling reversed Friday's gains, weighed by rising oil costs and a firmer dollar.
sections.why: UK snap election risk is building — polls show Labour's lead narrowing, and currency markets are pricing political uncertainty alongside the oil-driven inflation hit to the UK consumer.
sections.watch: UK employment data Wednesday 09:00 TRT — wage growth above 6% keeps the BoE boxed in; below 5.5% opens a dovish pivot.

headlines.title: Yen Spikes to 10-Week High and Sparks Intervention Chatter
UK Elections Threaten to End the Pound’s Resilient Run
Sterling Today: Pound jumps as dollar sells off on Iran-Hormuz deal hopes

sections.past: Dollar-yen pushed higher to 157.42 as US yields rose to 4.41%, pulling the pair away from Friday's intervention-fear lows.
sections.why: The BoJ's credibility problem deepens — 10Y JGB yields are stuck below 1.5% while the US-Japan spread widens, making intervention talk cheap without actual rate hikes.
sections.watch: Japan Q1 GDP Thursday 02:50 TRT — contraction print reopens the yen carry trade; positive surprise triggers another intervention scare above 158.

headlines.title: Why a Plunging Dollar Is Missing Out on the Relief Rally
How Markets Reacted to Trump's Iran Post, in Charts
The Dollar Isn’t Done. Why It’s Outshining Rivals Amid Iran Crisis.

sections.past: Aussie slipped to 0.7220 as commodity currencies softened despite surging oil — iron ore weakness and a firmer dollar outweighed energy gains.
sections.why: Australia's terms-of-trade sweet spot is fading as the Iran conflict drives oil costs higher without lifting iron ore or LNG prices proportionally.
sections.watch: RBA meeting minutes Tuesday — any hint of a hawkish hold due to oil-driven CPI pressures could stabilize AUD above 0.7200.

headlines.title: The Dollar is Now Lower Than When the Iran War Began
Dollar softens as Middle East ceasefire holds
Why is the Australian Dollar Rallying?

sections.past: Gold pulled back $14 to $4,705 despite escalating Iran tensions, as rising US yields and a stronger dollar capped upside after touching $4,783 intraday.
sections.why: The gold-yield tug-of-war is intensifying — geopolitical risk supports $4,600+ but 4.41% on the 10Y is the highest since March, creating real competition for safe-haven flows.
sections.watch: CPI Tuesday 15:30 TRT determines the next $100 move — hot inflation pushes yields higher and pressures gold; soft print sends it back toward $4,800.

headlines.title: Gold forecast and tracker: Where will prices land in 2026?
Barrick Mining Corp (B) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Record Cash Flow and Strategic Growth ...
Orla Mining Ltd (ORLA) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Strong Gold Production and Strategic ...

sections.past: Silver dropped to $84.98 after failing to hold above $88, underperforming gold as the gold-silver ratio widened back above 55.
sections.why: Silver's industrial demand story is being overshadowed by macro headwinds — rising yields hurt the zero-yield metal more than gold, and China PMI softness weighs on the industrial component.
sections.watch: Watch the $84 support — a break below opens $80; holding above $85 with a soft CPI print could reignite the push toward $90.

headlines.title: Gold and silver prices today, Monday, May 11: Gold and silver down this morning after Trump rejects Iran's peace plan
Oil climbs but markets shrug off US-Iran deadlock
K92 Mining Q1 Profit Jumps 66%, Revenue Hits Record on Higher Production and Prices

sections.past: WTI surged 2.5% to $100.49 after Trump declared the Iran ceasefire 'on life support' and rejected Tehran's counter-proposal, raising fears of renewed Strait of Hormuz disruption.
sections.why: Triple-digit oil is now the base case while Hormuz risk persists — even partial disruption to the strait's 21M bpd throughput would create a supply shock that no SPR release can offset.
sections.watch: EIA inventories Wednesday 17:30 TRT and any Hormuz shipping lane updates — draw above 3M barrels confirms demand resilience; tanker diversion news sends Brent toward $110.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Dow rises, S&P 500 and Nasdaq eke out records as chip stocks extend gains
How prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure could lead to oil demand destruction
Bitcoin and ethereum prices today, Monday, May 11, 2026: Bitcoin's strongest opening in months

sections.past: S&P 500 edged up 0.2% to a fresh record close at 7,412.84, powered by semis and AI plays despite oil crossing $100 and the VIX jumping to 18.4.
sections.why: The market is running a dangerous split — mega-cap tech keeps printing new highs on AI capex euphoria while breadth narrows and oil-sensitive sectors lag. Classic late-cycle divergence.
sections.watch: CPI Tuesday 15:30 TRT is the gatekeeper — consensus 3.4% YoY core; above 3.6% triggers a sell-the-news reaction; at or below consensus extends the grind to 7,500.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures slide as Wall Street braces for CPI report
Jerome Powell's 17 most memorable moments after leading the Federal Reserve for 8 eventful years
Massive AI investments come with one downside for Big Tech investors

sections.past: Nasdaq 100 gained 0.3% to 29,321 as memory chip stocks hit record highs led by Micron, and AI infrastructure plays continued their relentless bid.
sections.why: The AI capex cycle is entering its acceleration phase — hyperscaler spend commitments for 2026 now total $325B+, and the market is rewarding any company touching the inference stack.
sections.watch: CPI report Tuesday sets the tone; post-close focus shifts to any Tuesday pre-announcements ahead of May options expiry Friday.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures slide as Wall Street braces for CPI report
Why mid-cap stocks are stuck in 'no man's land'
Lumentum (LITE) Cracks Past $1,000—an All-Time High on Nasdaq 100 Inclusion

sections.past: Dow added 95 points to 49,704, with industrials and financials providing modest support while energy stocks outperformed on the oil surge.
sections.why: The Dow is lagging the Nasdaq by 15 percentage points YTD — a rotation into value would need lower yields and cooler oil, neither of which is materializing.
sections.watch: CPI Tuesday 15:30 TRT; 50K is the psychological magnet — a soft print could catalyze the breakout.

headlines.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures slide as Wall Street braces for CPI report
Up 377%, Is Intel Proving Why It Was a Mistake for Nvidia to Replace Intel in the Dow Jones Industrial Average?
Dow Jones Futures: Techs Fall As South Korea Eyes Excess AI Profits; CPI Inflation Due

sections.past: Bitcoin dipped 0.8% to $81,040 as crypto pulled back from Monday's strong open, with risk appetite fading as Trump's Iran comments rattled broader markets.
sections.why: BTC's correlation with risk assets is reasserting — despite the digital-gold narrative, bitcoin is trading more like a high-beta tech proxy as institutional flows dominate over retail.
sections.watch: CPI reaction in equities will set the tone for crypto; a move below $80,000 opens $77,500 support; holding $81K with equity strength targets $84,000.

MARA Holdings Inc (MARA) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Navigating Challenges with Strategic ...

sections.past: Ethereum fell 1.6% to $2,302 as the ETH/BTC ratio continued its multi-month slide, with Solana's developer share gains adding to bearish sentiment.
sections.why: Ethereum's fundamental problem is accelerating — Solana now commands 23% of active developers vs ETH's declining share, and the market is pricing in L1 competition eroding Ethereum's moat.
sections.watch: The $2,250 level is critical — a break below confirms the bearish trend toward $2,100; a bounce with volume above $2,350 signals a double-bottom.

headlines.title: Bitcoin and ethereum prices today, Monday, May 11, 2026: Bitcoin's strongest opening in months
Why Is The Crypto Market Down Today?
SharpLink Gaming Ltd. Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary

sections.past: NVIDIA surged 2.0% to $219.44, leading the chip rally as Intel's CEO highlighted collaborative products and memory stocks hit record highs.
sections.why: NVIDIA is the undisputed pick-and-shovel play of the AI boom — every hyperscaler's capex increase flows directly to Jensen's P&L, and the inference demand wave is just starting.
sections.watch: Watch for any pre-earnings guidance updates as we approach May earnings season; $222 is the near-term resistance from Monday's intraday high.

headlines.title: Intel stock rises as CEO Lip-Bu Tan touts 'exciting new products' with Nvidia
Memory chip stocks hit record highs, pharma reacts to hantavirus concerns
Arbe Robotics (ARBE) Expands Beyond Automotive Into Defense and Robotics Markets

sections.past: Tesla rocketed 3.9% to $445, its best day in three weeks, after Piper Sandler argued current prices give investors Optimus robotics exposure 'for free.'
sections.why: The bull case has fully shifted from cars to robots and autonomy — Piper's sum-of-parts valuation implies $0 for Optimus at current prices, which is the narrative driving momentum buyers.
sections.watch: Trump's China trip this week is critical — Musk is joining; any tariff relief signal for EV exports could push TSLA toward $460.

headlines.title: Tesla: Buying at these levels means you get Optimus for 'free', Piper Sandler says
Qualcomm China Trip Puts AI And Chip Export Risks In Focus
Jeff Bezos-Backed Slate Auto To Take On Tesla: Is Billionaire Now Distancing Himself From Startup?

sections.past: Apple was flat at $292.68 as the stock consolidated near highs ahead of the Trump China delegation trip, where Tim Cook is confirmed as a participant.
sections.why: Apple's China exposure is both its biggest risk and opportunity this week — 20% of revenue depends on a market where regulatory pressure and tariff uncertainty persist.
sections.watch: Any headlines from the Trump-Xi meeting on tech tariffs or App Store regulations will be the catalyst; $290 support, $298 resistance.

headlines.title: Tesla's Elon Musk, Apple's Tim Cook among CEOs set to join Trump on China trip
Memory chip stocks hit record highs, pharma reacts to hantavirus concerns
Qualcomm China Trip Puts AI And Chip Export Risks In Focus

sections.past: Microsoft slipped 0.6% to $412.66 as Nadella's testimony in the Musk-OpenAI trial dominated headlines, overshadowing otherwise solid fundamentals.
sections.why: The OpenAI trial is a distraction, not a fundamental risk — Microsoft's $13B investment is already generating returns through Azure AI, and no trial outcome changes that moat.
sections.watch: Trial headlines continue Tuesday; technically, $405 is the key support from Monday's low — a break below signals broader tech rotation.

headlines.title: Microsoft's Satya Nadella testifies in Elon Musk, OpenAI trial
Microsoft CEO says he was never given 'clarity' on why Altman was fired from OpenAI during Musk trial
Up 377%, Is Intel Proving Why It Was a Mistake for Nvidia to Replace Intel in the Dow Jones Industrial Average?

sections.past: Meta fell 1.8% to $598.86, breaking below $600 for the first time in two weeks as reports of an internal AI strategy crisis weighed on sentiment.
sections.why: The AI spending credibility gap is widening — Meta's $40B+ annual AI capex needs to show consumer product traction soon, and internal dissent about Zuckerberg's direction isn't helping.
sections.watch: The $595 level is the 20-day moving average support — a close below opens $580; above $605 and the dip gets bought.

headlines.title: By the numbers: Corporate profits haven't been this smoking hot in years
Circle CEO says AI-fueled layoffs are only the tip of the iceberg
Meta’s AI crisis deepens: Inside the fight against Mark Zuckerberg

sections.past: Alphabet dropped 3.0% to $388.64, the sharpest one-day decline since April, despite positive coverage of Anthropic's growth benefiting Google Cloud.
sections.why: The sell-off looks like profit-taking after a 30%+ YTD run rather than fundamental deterioration — but South Korea's proposed excess AI profit tax is creating a new regulatory overhang for the sector.
sections.watch: The $385 level is the 50-day moving average — a hold there confirms the dip is buyable; a break below signals a deeper correction to $370.

headlines.title: By the numbers: Corporate profits haven't been this smoking hot in years
Entergy Shareholders Back Board as CEO Highlights Data Center Demand, Grid Investments
Anthropic Just Delivered Great News for Alphabet Investors

sections.past: Amazon declined 1.4% to $268.99 as broader mega-cap weakness dragged the stock despite strong corporate profit environment and AI cloud demand.
sections.why: Amazon is caught in the crossfire of two narratives — AI cloud growth is accelerating, but the Trump China trip raises tariff uncertainty for the e-commerce business that still drives 60% of revenue.
sections.watch: Watch for China trade signals from the Trump delegation; $265 is the near-term support — a break below targets $258.
